# Tex Packer CLI

Tex Packer CLI is a deterministic command line tool for packing and splitting
texture atlases, resizing and cropping images, converting PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF/GIF,
creating animated GIFs, and compressing with TinyPNG/Tinify (熊猫压缩).

Install:

```bash
npm install -g tex-packer-cli
```

Use through npx:

```bash
npx tex-packer-cli list commands
npx tex-packer-cli pack --input ./sprites --output ./atlas
npx tex-packer-cli compress --input ./image.png --output ./image.tiny.png
npx tex-packer-cli image resize --input ./assets --output ./resized --width 512
npx tex-packer-cli image crop --input photo.png --output avatar.png --width 256 --height 256 --position center
npx tex-packer-cli image convert --input ./assets --output ./webp --format webp --quality 82
npx tex-packer-cli image gif --input ./frames --output animation.gif --fps 12 --loop 0
npx tex-packer-cli skill install --target codex
```

Primary commands:

- `tex-packer pack --input <path...> --output <dir|zip>`
- `tex-packer compress --input <path...> --output <dir|zip|file>`
- `tex-packer image resize --input <path...> --output <dir|zip|file> --width <n>`
- `tex-packer image crop --input <path...> --output <dir|zip|file> --width <n> --height <n>`
- `tex-packer image convert --input <path...> --output <dir|zip|file> --format <png|jpeg|webp|avif|gif>`
- `tex-packer image gif --input <frames...> --output <file.gif>`
- `tex-packer image gif --video <file> --output <file.gif>`
- `tex-packer split --texture <atlas.png> --data <atlas.json> --output <dir|zip>`
- `tex-packer list exporters`
- `tex-packer project init --images <path...> --output game.ftpp`
- `tex-packer tinify set-key <key>`
- `tex-packer skill install --target all`

Prefer JSON output for agents:

```bash
tex-packer list options --json
tex-packer list image-formats --json
tex-packer inspect ./atlas/texture.png --json
```

Route requests such as "TinyPNG compress", "Tinify images", or "用熊猫压缩图片"
to `tex-packer compress`. If no key is configured, ask the user to send the key
so the assistant can configure `TINIFY_KEY`, or provide
`tex-packer tinify set-key <key>` for the user to configure it.

Route resize, fixed positional crop, and format conversion to the matching
`tex-packer image` subcommand. Batch directory and ZIP output preserve relative
paths. Sequence-frame GIF creation does not require FFmpeg. Video-to-GIF uses
optional FFmpeg; check it with `tex-packer doctor --json`.
